news.summary: After working as the head of New York's social services department, Steven Banks expressed his desire to once again advocate for the rights of the homeless. However, he unexpectedly resigned shortly after an elite firm he was associated with finalized a deal with President Trump.
news.headline: Pro bono leader leaves Paul Weiss amid Trump crackdown on big law after deal made, citing return to homeless advocacy.

news.summary: In Aberdeen, Scotland, KNOT Offshore Partners LP (NYSE: KNOP) announced a quarterly cash distribution of US$0.026 per common unit for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The distribution will be payable on May 8, 2025, to all unitholders recorded by the close of business on April 28, 2025. The Partnership reiterates its confidence in the key elements of its strategy and valuation.
news.headline: KNOT Offshore Partners LP declares cash distribution of US$0.026 per common unit for Q1 2025, payable on May 8, 2025.

news.summary: Richardson Electronics reported a net sales increase for the third consecutive quarter with Q3 FY25 net sales reaching $53.8 million, a 2.7% growth YoY. The surge was mainly from Semiconductor wafer fab sales which climbed 139% and Canvys sales increasing by 39.5%. The company ended the quarter with $36.7 million in cash and equivalents after selling a majority of Richardson Healthcare. However, net loss was $2.1 million, although excluding one-time charges results in a non-GAAP operating income of $2.2 million, up from $1.0 million in the same period last year. EBITDA for the quarter was -$2.1 million, improving to $2.8 million after adjusting for the Healthcare asset sale. The Board of Directors declared a $0.06 quarterly cash dividend per share. Their nine-month fiscal summary indicated an increase in net sales to $157.0 million, a 5.3% growth YoY, with an operating loss of $3.1 million but a non-GAAP operating income of $1.8 million. Non-GAAP net income was $1.4 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $4.5 million for the nine-month period. In addition, financial measures were reported using both GAAP and non-GAAP metrics to provide a comprehensive view of the company’s performance.
news.headline: Richardson Electronics reports net sales of $53.8 million in Q3 FY25; operating loss of $2.7 million. Quarterly cash dividend declared.

news.summary: The president has increased tariffs on China even more, adding to the already high rates. He stated that Beijing's response to his previous trade measures was unwarranted.
news.headline: President Trump announces 90-day pause on global tariffs, increases tariffs on China in response to retaliation.

news.summary: In Jacksonville, Florida, Dun & Bradstreet, a prominent provider of business data and analytics, has unveiled its latest quarterly 2025 Global Business Optimism Insights report. The report shows a 1.3% decrease in business optimism compared to the previous quarter, which had experienced a significant 12.9% decline. This ongoing trend is attributed to uncertainties in the global economy and increasing trade protectionism, which are affecting business sentiment worldwide. Additionally, financial confidence has also decreased by 8.6%, reflecting the challenging economic conditions. The company is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol DNB.
news.headline: Global business optimism declines 1.3% quarter-over-quarter to 12.9%, due to trade policy uncertainties and high interest rates.

news.summary: QuantumScape Corporation, a pioneer in solid-state lithium-metal batteries (NYSE: QS), is set to unveil its 2025 Q1 business figures post-market on April 23, 2025. A conference call is scheduled at 2 p.m. PT (5 p.m. ET) on the same day with CEO Siva Sivaram and CFO Kevin Hettrich present. The announcement was made on April 9.
news.headline: QuantumScape to reveal Q1 2025 business results on April 23; CEO and CFO to host conference call at 2pm PT (5pm ET).

news.summary: A company called Horizon Technology Finance Corporation, affiliated with Monroe Capital and trading on NASDAQ under HRZN, shared its Q1 2025 portfolio update. news.summary: A company headquartered in Henderson, Nevada named Paysign, Inc. will be participating in the Ladenburg Thalmann Technology Innovation Expo 25. news.summary: BWX Technologies, Inc. disclosed today that it has received a contract from the U.S. Department of Energy to oversee and run the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. This responsibility will be carried out by Strategic Storage Partners, LLC, a joint venture between APTIM Federal Services and BWXT Technical Services Group, Inc. The contract is valued at around $2.6 billion and spans a 10-year period. The agreement consists of a five-year initial term, with the option to extend for an additional five years.
news.headline: BWXT, APTIM, and Strategic Storage Partners awarded $2.6B contract to manage Strategic Petroleum Reserve structures and systems.

news.summary: Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. announced today that their preliminary assets under management as of March 31, 2025 were $162.4 billion. Of this total, $79.2 billion came from Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds, while $83.2 billion originated from separate accounts and other AUM. The company's AUM composition highlights a balanced distribution between fund-based and other managed assets, showcasing a robust financial portfolio.
news.headline: Artisan Partners Asset Management reported $162.4 billion AUM as of March 31, 2025, with Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds totaling $79.2 billion.

news.summary: Madison Square Garden Entertainment Corp. disclosed on April 9, 2025, that David Collins, a seasoned financial executive with a background in live entertainment and sports, will be joining the company. Collins brings a wealth of experience and expertise to MSG Entertainment, listed on the NYSE under the symbol MSGE. This move is expected to leverage Collins' industry knowledge and strategic acumen for the company's growth in the competitive market.
news.headline: MSG Entertainment appoints David Collins as Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer. #NYSE: MSGE #April925.

news.summary: In Denver on April 9, 2025, Antero Resources (NYSE: AR) shared that they will release their first quarter 2025 earnings on April 30, 2025, after NYSE trading hours. They will hold a conference call to discuss these earnings.

news.summary: President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on "reciprocal tariffs" and reduced tariffs to 10% for countries not imposing counter-tariffs. However, tariffs on China were raised to 125% due to their retaliatory measures. This led the S&P 500 index to surge by almost 7%. Trump's tariff decisions have caused extreme volatility in the markets, with countless fluctuations in stock values. The volatility index exceeded 60, showcasing the highest levels since August 2024. Despite dropping to 37.5, it indicates ongoing market turmoil. Arthur Hayes, BitMEX founder, suggested that the devaluation of the Chinese yuan in response to tariffs could drive investment towards cryptocurrencies. This news highlights the unpredictable nature of global trade policies and their impact on financial markets.
news.headline: Trump pauses some tariffs, boosts China. 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs, 10% rate on non-retaliatory countries, 125% hike on China. S&P 500 index surges 7%. Market experiencing extreme volatility. Volatility index at 37.5. Devaluation of yuan may drive capital into crypto. DeFi predicted to rise post-memecoins.

news.headline: Invesco reports $1,844.8 billion AUM as of March 31, 2025, with $6.5 billion net long-term inflows.

news.summary: Midland States Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: MSBI) from Effingham, Illinois, received a deficiency notification letter from Nasdaq on April 3, 2025, due to non-compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(c)(1). The company failed to timely file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. This information was disclosed in the Form 12b-25 filed with the SEC on March 17, 2025. Nasdaq Listing Rule mandates timely filing of all required periodic reports with the SEC.
news.headline: Midland States Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: MSBI) receives Nasdaq deficiency notice for delayed Form 10-K filing on April 3, 2025.

news.summary: A recent study revealed that over 1,500 new cases of the virus have been reported in the past 24 hours. The total number of confirmed cases has now surpassed 30,000, with a significant increase in the number of deaths, totaling more than 500. Hospitals are becoming overwhelmed with patients, leading to concerns about the healthcare system's capacity. The government has implemented strict measures to curb the spread of the virus, including mandatory mask-wearing and limitations on public gatherings. Experts warn that the situation may worsen in the coming weeks if proper precautions are not taken.
news.headline: Stocks surge as Trump postpones tariffs | Latest from Bloomberg's Balance of Power: Early Edition.
Today, Trump advised buying, and it was a wise move.

news.summary: Ameresco, Inc., a top energy solutions provider traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol AMRC, has recently confirmed a design-build agreement worth $11 million with Reed College in Portland, Oregon. The contract encompasses a wide-ranging upgrade of the college's boiler plant, including mechanical, electrical, and seismic enhancements. This deal underscores the strong and enduring partnership between Ameresco and Reed College, showcasing their continued collaboration and commitment to sustainable energy solutions.
news.headline: Ameresco secures $11M contract with Reed College for energy upgrades, strengthening their partnership.
